div.h2: Entry/Exit Output Assembly: One strace Line Across Two ptrace Stops

ul.li: write(fd, buf, n) where n ≤ PIPE_BUF (4096 bytes) is atomic for pipes and FIFOs; for regular files (including /dev/stderr pointing to a terminal), the kernel serializes within a single process because the tracer is single-threaded — no concurrent writer exists. One fputs() → one write() syscall → no split.
A complete strace line is always ≤ 512 bytes (outbuf size).

Single-threaded tracer invariant: waitpid(-1) serializes all PID events; only one handle_syscall_stop() executes at a time. The buffer is the guard against logical interleaving across entry/exit stops.

g.text:
snprintf → outbuf = "[pid 1234] open(\"/etc/passwd\", O_RDONLY, 0644"
outbuf_len = 44
NO newline written yet
NO fputs yet
PTRACE_SYSCALL(pid, 0, 0) → tracee resumes; kernel executes open()
